title: echeveria racemosa var. citrina
intro: Echeveria racemosa var. citrina, also known as the Lemon Rock Rose or Sunshine Echeveria, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to support the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as an infusion or tincture, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
